zotero.abstractNote: We present GraphDreamer, a framework for compositional 3D scene synthesis from scene graphs. Given a scene graph as input, our method generates a 3D scene by optimizing a set of 3D assets and their spatial arrangements. To achieve this, we propose a novel compositional 3D representation that allows for independent optimization of individual objects while maintaining global scene consistency. We leverage a pre-trained 2D diffusion model as a prior to guide the optimization process, ensuring that the generated scenes are both visually plausible and faithful to the input scene graph. Our experiments demonstrate that GraphDreamer can generate high-quality 3D scenes with complex spatial relationships and diverse object categories, outperforming existing methods in both visual quality and structural fidelity.
zotero.conferenceName: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
zotero.proceedingsTitle: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
